name: Sierra de Tablón y El Terril — circular desde Algámitas
name_original: Sierra de Tablón y El Terril — circular desde  Algámitas
region_intro: Jogging routes around Algámitas traverse a mountainous landscape in the Sierra Sur of Seville, Spain. The region features prominent limestone formations such as El Peñón de Algámitas and Pico del Terril, the highest point in Seville province. Running trails often pass through Mediterranean forests of holm oaks and cork oaks, as well as extensive olive groves. The Corbones River also provides routes along its banks, offering varied terrain for runners. Best jogging routes around Algámitas The most popular jogging route is Sierra de Tablón y El Terril — circular desde Algámitas, a 11.8 miles (19.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local runners is Cordel de Morón and Peñón de Algámitas Loop, a difficult 9.3 miles (15.0 km) path. This route winds through the Sierra del Tablón, featuring rugged terrain and scenic vistas. Local runners also love the Views of Algámitas – Peñón de Algámitas loop from Restaurante Mirador, a 6.2 miles (9.9 km) trail leading through varied landscapes with views of El Peñón, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. Jogging around Algámitas is defined by mountainous terrain, limestone peaks, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging ascents to more moderate paths. The routes in Algámitas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 3 reviews. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Algámitas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Algámitas?
A: Algámitas offers a diverse selection of over 15 running routes. These trails traverse the mountainous landscape of the Sierra Sur of Seville, providing varied options for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Algámitas?
A: While many routes in Algámitas are rated as difficult due to the mountainous terrain and significant elevation changes, there are also more moderate paths available. For a less strenuous experience, consider exploring the lower sections of trails or the extensive olive groves which offer a gentler relief.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails around Algámitas?
A: The jogging trails in Algámitas feature a mix of mountainous terrain, prominent limestone formations like El Peñón and Pico del Terril, and Mediterranean forests. You'll run through holm oaks, cork oaks, and extensive olive groves, with some routes also following the banks of the Corbones River.

Q: Can I find circular jogging routes in Algámitas?
A: Yes, Algámitas is known for its excellent circular running routes. A popular option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012728868/sierra-de-tablon-y-el-terril-circular-desde-algamitas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Tablón y El Terril — circular desde Algámitas&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 19 km and offers panoramic views. Another great loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012720731/cordel-de-moron-and-penon-de-algamitas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cordel de Morón and Peñón de Algámitas Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 15 km path through rugged terrain.

Q: Are there any jogging routes with panoramic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely! The region is famous for its breathtaking vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31433981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Algámitas – Peñón de Algámitas loop from Restaurante Mirador&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase the scenery, offering stunning views of El Peñón. Ascents to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4048502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Terril&lt;/a&gt; also provide unparalleled panoramic views over Seville's olive groves and the surrounding area.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while jogging in Algámitas?
A: While jogging in Algámitas, you'll encounter impressive natural landmarks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391389" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Peñón de Algámitas&lt;/a&gt;, a prominent rock formation, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4048502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Terril&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point in the province of Seville. The routes also pass through beautiful Mediterranean forests and along the Corbones River, particularly enchanting in the 'Bosque de las Hadas' (Fairy Forest) during spring.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Algámitas?
A: The running routes in Algámitas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the challenging ascents to peaks like El Terril, and the stunning panoramic views from the limestone formations. The peaceful rural setting and diverse natural features are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are the jogging trails in Algámitas suitable for families?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, some sections and the extensive olive groves offer gentler paths that can be suitable for families looking for a less strenuous activity. For a dedicated family-friendly option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1043791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, a flatter, old railway line path a short drive away, ideal for walking and cycling with children.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running trails in Algámitas?
A: Many of the natural trails and rural paths around Algámitas are suitable for running with dogs, provided they are kept under control and local regulations are followed. The diverse landscapes, from olive groves to forest paths, offer plenty of space for you and your canine companion to enjoy. Always ensure your dog is well-hydrated, especially on longer or more challenging routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Algámitas?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best times for jogging in Algámitas. The temperatures are milder, and the natural landscape is particularly vibrant. Spring brings lush greenery and wildflowers, especially along the Corbones River, while autumn offers pleasant weather and beautiful seasonal colors. Summers can be very hot, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended if visiting during this season.

Q: Are there any historical points of interest along the running routes?
A: Yes, the region has a rich history. The 'Ruta de los Bandoleros' (Bandit's Route) explores caves in &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pe%C3%B1%C3%B3n_de_Alg%C3%A1mitas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Peñón&lt;/a&gt; where ancient outlaws once hid, offering a unique blend of exercise and local history. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5339186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Remedies&lt;/a&gt; is a notable religious building in the area.

Q: Are there any easier, shorter running loops available?
A: For a shorter, yet still scenic, option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38214418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Terril – Sierra de Tablón Pass loop from Puerto de la Ventana&lt;/a&gt;. This route is approximately 6.5 km and offers a good workout with significant elevation gain, but is shorter than the full ascents to the peaks.
tips: Pico del Terril is the highest altitude in the Spanish province of Seville in Andalusia, at 1,129 meters above sea level. It is located on the border line between the terms of the towns of Algámitas and Pruna, on the summit of the hill known as "El Monigote" which is the highest in the Sierra del Tablón.

Collado Sierra de Tablón is a highlight on the circular route that includes Sierra de Tablón and El Terril, near Algámitas, in the province of Seville. This route is known for its difficulty, requiring good physical fitness and experience in high mountains.
